design.description: Search validation for the Purchase Order screen in FMT OS, including no-match behavior across tabs and input edge cases.

flows.name: Search bar visibility and input acceptance
steps: User opens Purchase Order screen
Search bar is visible
User types a non-matching search string
Entered text remains visible in the input

flows.name: Non-matching search across all tabs
steps: User types random non-matching text in the search bar
Review PO shows 'No purchase orders to review'
Map SO shows 'No purchase orders to map'
In Progress shows 'No purchase orders in progress'
Completed shows 'No completed purchase orders'
All shows 'No purchase orders found'
Search value remains applied across all tab switches

flows.name: Search from non-default tab
steps: User opens the Map SO tab
User types random non-matching text
Map SO remains the active tab
Map SO empty state is displayed

flows.name: Special-character search
steps: User types '@#$%' in the search bar
Input is accepted
Screen remains stable
Review PO empty state is displayed

flows.name: Long-text search
steps: User types a long search string
Input is accepted
Screen remains stable
Review PO empty state is displayed

flows.name: Whitespace-only search
steps: User types only whitespace in the search bar
Screen remains stable
All tabs remain visible
Review PO remains active

flows.name: Clear search behavior
steps: User types random non-matching text
User clears the search input
Search bar becomes empty
All tabs remain visible

flows.name: Leading/trailing spaces are trimmed
steps: User types text with spaces at both ends
Search input value is trimmed by the system
Expected no-data message is still shown on active tab

business_rules: Search operates within the currently active Purchase Order tab.
A non-matching search should show the empty state relevant to the active tab.
Search text should persist in the search input while switching tabs unless it is cleared by the user.
Search should safely handle special characters, long text, and whitespace-only input.
Clearing the search input should reset the input state without breaking the Purchase Order screen.
Leading and trailing spaces in search input should be trimmed.
validations: Search bar must be visible on the Purchase Order screen.
Search bar value must match the user-entered input.
Expected empty-state primary message must match the active tab.
Active tab should remain correct when searching from a non-default tab.
All tabs should remain visible after whitespace-only search and after clearing search.
Search input should remove leading/trailing spaces from text values.
edge_cases: Clearing search after a no-match query

selector_hints.suggested_selector: input[placeholder='Search by PO#, Supplier Name & ID']
